extracted_information: The article emphasizes the critical need for trusted age verification systems in the rapidly growing legal cannabis market to prevent underage individuals (under 21) from obtaining products. It highlights the inadequacies of traditional methods like manual ID checks and online 'age gates', which are easily circumvented. The proposed solution involves advanced biometric technology for remote age verification, ensuring compliance, enhancing security, and streamlining the customer experience. Non-compliance can lead to severe penalties for dispensaries, including fines, incarceration, and license revocation.

specifications.age_requirement: 21 years and above for cannabis sales
specifications.traditional_id_check_limitations: Relies on untrained store clerks, difficulty assessing forged IDs
specifications.online_age_gate_limitations: Easily bypassed by lying about age, allows multiple attempts, no proof validation
specifications.recommended_technology: Facial biometric identity verification and automated identity document authentication
specifications.biometric_process: User takes a selfie with a camera-enabled mobile device, completes anti-spoofing active liveness detection check, snapshots driver’s license for automated authentication
specifications.identity_document_authentication_capabil: Validates genuineness of ID, provides real-time data on birthday and age, supports 1,500+ government IDs and non-chip passports from 200 countries, includes connections to government registries, and American, Canadian, or Mexican driver’s license authentication.
specifications.post_verification_efficiency: Eliminates purchase and delivery friction as re-verification is not needed for subsequent purchases after initial onboarding

features: Validation of claimed age and identity
Automatic confirmation of authenticity of presented government-issued credentials
Biometric matching of selfie to verified credentials
Real-time age and birthday data provision

geographical_data.legal_cannabis_states_2021: 16 states (including Washington, D.C.) for adult-use; 37 states for medical-use
geographical_data.ID_document_coverage: 200 countries for non-chip passports, American, Canadian, or Mexican driver’s licenses

references.description: Forbes article on US legal pot industry
Study on age gates in alcohol websites
